Offline phone handbook

Nick's "Useful Japanese" Handbook!

Sorted by use case, searchable by Japanese, kana, typo-tolerant romaji, or English. Built-ins stay read-only; your own additions live in this browser and can be exported as JSON.

あいう かきく 日本語
0 shown 0 built in 0 mine 0 saved

Add Word Or Phrase

Custom entries are stored only in this browser until you export them. Required fields match the AI schema so your additions stay portable.

Optional but useful for AI workflows. Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.
Kanji, katakana, and/or hiragana display form.
Optional for custom entries. Built-ins are generated automatically.
Advanced AI fields
Optional. Use an array of objects with term, meaning, and optional ref entry id.
Optional comma-separated ids. Cross-reference ids are shown inside entry cards.

JSON Schema

Every entry uses this stable shape. Built-ins can be edited locally without changing the embedded source data; imported entries are treated as custom entries.

Example Object

AI Interaction Notes

  • Export your personal data, ask an AI model to edit the JSON, then import the result. Personal exports include custom entries, local built-in edits, notes, and tags.
  • Search matches Japanese, kana, English, spaced romaji, no-space romaji, example romaji, common long-vowel variants such as arigato for arigatou, and small romaji typos such as Ohaiyo for ohayou.
  • Keep ids stable. Use lowercase ASCII ids like custom-mizu-o-kudasai.
  • Use plain Hepburn-style romaji without macrons: arigatou, shou shou, koohii.
  • Use romajiParts and exampleRomajiParts with matching text, kana, and romaji chunks when token color alignment matters.
  • Kana and romaji tokens can be hovered for a compact definition. The app uses full entries first, then an internal token glossary fallback. Add standalone entries, or optional forms, when you want those tokens to open richer solo cards.
  • Built-in examples are checked during generation so each displayed Japanese token resolves to a standalone entry or a documented form.
  • Sentence helper API: call window.JapaneseHandbook.buildRomajiSentence("watashi nihongo suki?") to preview suggested particles and question glue.
  • Browser API: call window.JapaneseHandbook.exportPersonal() to inspect the same portable personal-data payload used by Export Mine.
  • Do not auto-color natural English by word number. Use englishParts or exampleEnglishParts only for manually aligned gloss chunks.
  • Use exampleNotes for uncommon words in examples. Add ref when the note points to another listed entry.
  • Use relatedEntryIds sparingly for strong links, not every vaguely similar phrase.

Suggested Prompts

Clean this Japanese handbook JSON. Preserve each id, category, and user intent. Fix unnatural examples, add concise quirks, and return only valid JSON.
Add 20 standard modern Japanese entries for the category "travel". Use the documented schema, include kana, plain romaji, quirks, examples, and exampleNotes.
Review these custom entries for tone. Mark any phrase that sounds too blunt, old-fashioned, masculine-coded, feminine-coded, or customer-service formal.

Import Format

Import either an array of entries or an object with entries or customEntries. Personal exports can also contain builtInOverrides and annotations. Invalid entries are skipped and reported.

My Data

Custom entries, local built-in edits, notes, tags, and favorites stay in this browser. Export them before moving to another device or asking an AI model to help revise them.

Built as a portable offline handbook. Language notes favor standard modern Japanese and conversational usefulness over textbook order.
